During the season, they competed in the [[2023 Super League season]] and the [[2023 Challenge Cup]]. Prior to the sacking of [[Tony Smith (rugby league, born 1967)|Tony Smith]] as head coach on 4 July 2022 and resultant temporary appointment of [[Danny McGuire]] for the rest of the [[2022 Hull Kingston Rovers season|2022 season]], Hull Kingston Rovers announced in May 2022 the appointment of former [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] [[Halfback (rugby league)|halfback]] and [[Newcastle Knights]] assistant coach [[Willie Peters]] as the team's head coach for the 2023 season in a three-year deal.{{cite news |last1=Ibbetson |first1=Stephen |title=Paul Lakin explains appointment of Willie Peters as Hull KR head coach |url=https://www.totalrl.com/paul-lakin-explains-appointment-of-willie-peters-as-hull-kr-head-coach/ |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=Total Rugby League |date=10 May 2022}} ==Preseason friendlies== {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col3center col6center col9right" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!Report |- | 20 January; 17:45{{efn|[[Craig Hall (rugby league, born 1988)|Craig Hall]]'s testimonial match}} | {{leagueicon|Featherstone|16}} [[Featherstone Rovers]] | A | [[Post Office Road]] | {{WinDL|res=L}} | 0–28 | | | 2,976 |{{cite news |last1=Appleyard |first1=Joe |title=Hull KR verdict with Robins nilled by Featherstone Rovers in first hit out |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-verdict-featherstone-rovers-8054073 |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=20 January 2023}} |- | 5 February, 15:00 | {{leagueicon|Leeds|16}} [[Leeds Rhinos]] | H | [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] | {{WinDL|res=W}} | 26–4 | [[Ryan Hall (rugby league)|R. [[Channel 4]] |{{cite news |last1=Appleyard |first1=Joe |title=Hull KR's season comes to brutal end as Wigan Warriors thrash Robins in semi-final |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-wigan-warriors-report-8809728 |access-date=7 August 2025 |date=7 October 2023}} |} ==Challenge Cup== {{main|2023 Challenge Cup}} [[File:2023 Challenge Cup Final Match Underway 01.jpg|thumb|Hull Kingston Rovers in action against the [[Leigh Leopards]] at [[Wembley Stadium]] during the [[2023 Challenge Cup Final]]]] Hull Kingston Rovers were first drawn on 24 April to play the [[Batley Bulldogs]] at home in Round 6, confirmed following Batley's Round 5 34-16 victory against the [[Keighley Cougars]] the day prior.{{cite news |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Challenge Cup sixth round draw made |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/challenge-cup-sixth-round-draw-made |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=Love Rugby League |date=24 April 2023}} Following a 50-0 victory against Batley,{{cite news |title=Challenge Cup: Hull KR 50-0 Batley Bulldogs - Rovers romp into last eight |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/65615417 |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=19 May 2023}} Hull Kingston Rovers entered the quarter-finals and were drawn at home to the [[Salford Red Devils]].{{cite news |last1=Appleyard |first1=Joe |title=Hull KR's Challenge Cup quarter-final details as time and date announced |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-challenge-cup-salford-8465552 |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=24 May 2023}} After winning 28-10 against Salford,{{cite news |last1=Appleyard |first1=Joe |title=Hull KR talking points as Robins prove doubters wrong in style with Wembley statement |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-kr-salford-talking-points-8530218 |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=Hull Daily Mail |date=17 June 2023}} Hull Kingston Rovers were drawn into the semi-finals against league leaders [[Wigan Warriors]], to be played at the neutral [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].{{cite news |last1=Walker |first1=Callum |title=Men and Women's Challenge Cup semi-final draw revealed |url=https://www.totalrl.com/men-and-womens-challenge-cup-semi-final-draw-revealed/ |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=Total Rugby League |date=18 June 2023}} A contentious match saw Hull Kingston Rovers win the match in extra time on [[Golden point#Rugby League|golden point]] rules with a [[drop goal]] by [[Brad Schneider (rugby league)|Brad Schneider]], sending the club to the [[2023 Challenge Cup final|Challenge Cup final]] for the first time since their 50-0 defeat to [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[2015 Challenge Cup final|2015 final]].{{cite news |last1=Harby |first1=Chris |title=Challenge Cup: Hull KR 11-10 Wigan Warriors - Robins reach final with golden-point win |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66246093 |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=23 July 2023}} Hull Kingston Rovers emerged as runners-up at [[Wembley Stadium]] against the [[Leigh Leopards]], who won the match in extra time on golden point rules with a drop goal by [[Lachlan Lam]].{{cite news |last1=Newsum |first1=Matt |title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438 |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=BBC Sport |date=12 August 2023}} The 2023 final was the first time a Challenge Cup final had been held without either Leeds Rhinos, Wigan Warriors, [[Warrington Wolves]] or [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]] participating since 1986.{{cite news |last1=Pattison |first1=Cam |title=Revealed: Where 2023 Challenge Cup Final ranks compared to every final since 2001 |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/revealed-where-2023-challenge-cup-final-ranks-compared-to-every-final-since-2001-80124/ |access-date=6 August 2025 |work=Serious About Rugby League |date=14 August 2023}} {{table alignment}} {| class="wikitable defaultleft col4center col7center col10right" style="font-size:85%;" !Date and time!!Round!!Versus!!H/A!!Venue!!Result!!Score!!Tries!!Goals!!Attendance!!TV!!Report |- | 19 May; 20:00 | Round 6 | {{leagueicon|Batley|16}} [[Batley Bulldogs]] | H | [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] | {{WinDL|res=W}} | 50–0 | [[Ethan Ryan|Ryan]] (3), [[Kane Linnett|Linnett]] (2), [[Shaun Kenny-Dowall|Kenny-Dowall]] (2), [[Mikey Lewis|Lewis]], [[Sam Luckley|Luckley]] | [[Mikey Lewis|Lewis]] (5/7), [[Sam Wood (rugby league, born 1997)|Wood]] (2/2) | 5,143 | Not televised | |- | 17 June; 12:00 | Quarter-finals |{{leagueicon|Salford city|16}} [[Salford Red Devils]] | H | [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]] | {{WinDL|res=W}} | 28–10 | [[Ryan Hall (rugby league)|R. Hall]], [[Tanguy Zenon|Zenon]], [[Kane Linnett|Linnett]], [[Shaun Kenny-Dowall|Kenny-Dowall]], [[Dean Hadley|Hadley]] | [[Rowan Milnes|Milnes]] (4/5) | 6,289 | [[Viaplay Sports]] 1 | |- | 23 July; 17:00 | Semi-finals | {{leagueicon|Wigan|16}} [[Wigan Warriors]] | N | [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]] | {{WinDL|res=W}} | 11–10 {{goldenpt}} | [[Louis Senior|Senior]], [[Ethan Ryan|Ryan]] | [[Brad Schneider (rugby league)|Schneider]] (1/2)
